parsed.reason: This answer is exemplary. It normalizes developmentally appropriate behavior (toddler transition difficulty), cites neurology/brain development as the framework explaining why meltdowns occur at this stage, and offers concrete, evidence-based parenting strategies (transition warnings, goodbye rituals) without medical advice. The explanation of *why* each strategy works empowers the parent to understand their child and adapt flexibly. No red flags present; appropriate scope for a parenting coach.
